trivia_bullets.text: The name derives from 'Ilysia' — THB's heroic afterlife realm described in the flavor text — and 'caryatid,' a real Greek architectural term for a sculpted female figure serving as a load-bearing column. A caryatid is literally a human body pressed into structural service. On Theros, the Caryatid cards animate this concept: a living plant-being shaped like the ancient supporting-column figures. Ilysia mirrors the Elysian Fields of Greek myth (abode of the heroic dead), making this card a literal nature-pillar guarding the heroic paradise. [Scryfall flavor_text: thb/174; MTG Salvation forums: THS Block real-life influences thread, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: MTG Salvation forums: THS Block real-life influences, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Those who die as heroes are permitted to dwell in Ilysia, a protected realm of the Underworld as tranquil and vibrant as the rest is bleak." Ilysia is THB's version of the Elysian Fields — a Underworld paradise enclosed within Erebos's domain but insulated from its torments. The caryatid stands guard over this boundary. [Scryfall flavor_text: thb/174]

Ilysian Caryatid holds EDHREC rank 1,753 — remarkably high for a common, placing it in the top tier of Theros Beyond Death commons by Commander adoption. Its mana ability doubles output when you control a creature with power 4 or greater, making it a strong ramp piece in big-creature green strategies. It has been reprinted three times: Arena Beginner Set (2020), Jumpstart 2022, and Commander Masters (2023) — confirming Wizards's assessment of it as format-stable and beginner-appropriate. [Scryfall edhrec_rank; Scryfall prints search: oracleid 5f2be3c2]

Ilysian Caryatid follows directly from Sylvan Caryatid (Theros, 2013) — the earlier Theros-block plant mana creature. The design pattern of a 'living column' mana dork is a Theros-specific creature concept. Sylvan Caryatid was a staple in Standard 2014-2015 for its hexproof + fixing; Ilysian Caryatid trades hexproof for the power-4 doubling clause, shifting its ceiling higher in large-creature decks. Both are illustrated in the same column-guardian aesthetic, with Ilysian's art by Winona Nelson placing the figure in the Ilysia paradise landscape. [Scryfall: ths/180 Sylvan Caryatid; Scryfall: thb/174 artist field]
